fields.label: Phone
fields.helper: The hiring team may use this number to contact you about this job.

fields.label: These questions replace the cover letter. Please note: AI-assisted submissions are acceptable if they are thoughtfully edited and reflect your real experience—generic responses may be excluded.

Are you based in one of these cities: Detroit, Cleveland, Columbus, 
Cincinnati, Atlanta (Georgia)?
Why are you interested in this program and role?

What does community organizing mean to you?
What are your go-to organizing tools and/or tactics that you use and how have you used those in the past?

How do you currently stay connected to your community? 
In what ways are you connected and involved in your community beyond formal professional experience?
Have you ever used any canvassing tools or field organizing software/platforms? Which have you used, if any?
Even you haven’t used any, what’s your preferred method to track outreach details?
What aspects of voter outreach are most useful for you to track or have visualized in reaching your outreach goals?
If you were referred to apply to this role, who were you referred by?

This position runs from August through mid-November.  The hours are flexible, but the position requires up to 20 hours/week, frequently on evenings or weekends. Is that a schedule you can commit to, and what is your general availability?
Are you legally authorized to work in the US?
